text: hey sofia, I pulled the latest activation funnel numbers — we're seeing a 38% drop-off rate between signup and completing the onboarding checklist. the first-time experience is clearly not working. want to sync this week to scope the onboarding redesign?

the onboarding drop-off numbers are really bad — 38% of users bail before completing the checklist. we need to rethink the whole welcome screen. progressive disclosure instead of dumping everything on the user at once. quinn and I are putting together an onboarding spec this week

@jack_brown we're kicking off the onboarding redesign — can you start sketching wireframes for a new welcome screen? main issue is 38% drop-off in the activation funnel before users complete the checklist. thinking wizard-style flow with progressive disclosure instead of the current wall-of-text approach

love it. I've been wanting to redesign that first-time experience for a while. the empty states are also really confusing for new users — they land on a blank dashboard with no guidance. let me put together some user research findings I had from last quarter and map out the flow

heads up team — we're officially starting the onboarding redesign project. current activation funnel shows a 38% drop-off between signup and checklist completion. @sofia_martinez, @jack_brown, @grace_park, and @jordan_kim will be on this. goal is to ship a new first-time experience with better progressive disclosure and a proper onboarding checklist by end of month

starting wireframes for the onboarding redesign — rethinking the welcome screen, empty states, and the whole first-time user flow. if anyone on design has user research data about new user confusion points, send it my way. want to nail the product tour and tooltip placement
saw the onboarding redesign kickoff — excited to work on this. do we have a rough idea of what components I should start thinking about? checklist widget, wizard steps, tooltips?

yeah the main components will be: an onboarding checklist sidebar, a welcome screen modal, tooltip overlay system for the product tour, and improved empty states for all the key pages. jack is working on wireframes now, should have something for you by wednesday
hey mike! quinn mentioned I'll be working on the onboarding redesign project. where can I find the onboarding spec and any existing docs? still getting oriented with the codebase

welcome aboard! the onboarding spec is in the product wiki under /projects/onboarding-redesign. for the codebase, the current onboarding flow lives in src/features/onboarding/ — it's a bit messy but grace_park can walk you through the component structure. don't hesitate to ping me with questions

hi everyone! I'm jordan, just started two weeks ago. excited to be joining the onboarding redesign project. still getting up to speed — is there a recording of the kickoff meeting I can watch to catch up?

welcome jordan! yes, the recording is in the shared drive under onboarding-redesign/meetings. the main takeaway is we need to fix the activation funnel — too many new users drop off before completing the onboarding checklist. ask @mike_torres if you need help finding anything

first round of wireframes done for the onboarding redesign welcome screen — went with a 3-step wizard: (1) team setup, (2) first project creation, (3) guided product tour. each step has contextual tooltips. also redesigned the empty states so they have actionable CTAs instead of just 'nothing here yet'. link in Figma, feedback welcome

this is great. the wizard approach with progressive disclosure is exactly what I was hoping for. one thought — can we add an onboarding checklist that persists in the sidebar so users can track their progress even after dismissing the wizard? research shows visible progress indicators improve completion rates

totally — persistent checklist makes sense. I'll add it to the wireframes. should be a collapsible sidebar component that shows completion percentage and remaining steps. let me also add some empty state variations for the dashboard, projects page, and team page

jack's wireframes for the onboarding redesign are in the Figma link — can you take a look and estimate effort for the checklist component, welcome screen wizard, and tooltip overlay? want to put together a rough sprint plan

looked through jack's onboarding redesign wireframes — here's my rough eng estimate: onboarding checklist sidebar ~2 days, welcome screen wizard ~3 days with animations, tooltip overlay system for the product tour 3-4 days, empty states ~1 day. roughly 2 weeks total. @quinn_davis does that fit the timeline?
how's the onboarding redesign shaping up? I need to update the exec team on user activation efforts. do we have a target for improving the drop-off rate?

onboarding redesign status: jack finished wireframes — wizard-based welcome screen with progressive disclosure, persistent onboarding checklist, redesigned empty states, and a guided product tour with tooltips. grace estimated ~2 weeks of eng work. our target is reducing the activation funnel drop-off from 38% to under 20%. @sofia_martinez sharing this for the exec update

perfect. that's ambitious but achievable. make sure we have analytics instrumented at each step of the wizard so we can see exactly where users drop off in the new flow. I'll present this to the exec team thursday

hey grace, mike pointed me to src/features/onboarding/ for the current onboarding flow. I'm trying to understand how the checklist state is managed — is it stored in local storage or does it persist to the backend? just want to make sure I understand before I start looking at the new onboarding checklist component

good question! right now it's local storage only, which is part of the problem — if users switch devices they lose their progress. the new onboarding checklist will need to persist to the backend via the user preferences API. I can walk you through the component structure tomorrow if you want
that would be super helpful, thanks! I'll review the user preferences API docs beforehand so I'm not going in totally blind

posted updated wireframes for the onboarding redesign in Figma — welcome screen wizard, onboarding checklist sidebar, empty state redesigns for dashboard/projects/team pages, and the product tour tooltip system. would love feedback from anyone who has bandwidth. @lisa_chen could the wizard step transitions use some motion design love?

@jack_brown quick question on the wireframes — for the tooltip system in the product tour, are you thinking we anchor tooltips to specific UI elements or do a full-screen spotlight approach? the implementation is quite different for each

anchored tooltips with a subtle backdrop dimming. the spotlight approach felt too intrusive in user research — people just wanted to skip through it. anchored tooltips with progressive disclosure let users engage at their own pace. I'll add more detail on positioning logic in the wireframes

onboarding redesign update: wireframes are finalized for the welcome screen wizard, checklist, empty states, and product tour. @grace_park is starting implementation next week. target is cutting the activation funnel drop-off rate in half. @ryan_nguyen can you set up a dashboard so we can track the new onboarding funnel metrics once it ships?

heads up — starting on the onboarding redesign implementation next week. main pieces: welcome screen wizard, onboarding checklist sidebar component, tooltip overlay system for the product tour, and new empty states. if anyone has built a tooltip positioning system before I'd love to chat — want to avoid reinventing the wheel

one more thing for the onboarding spec — can you add a section on what happens when users complete the checklist? I'm thinking a celebratory empty state that transitions to the normal dashboard. small detail but the first-time experience should end on a high note

great call — I'll add a completion state to the onboarding spec. thinking confetti animation + a 'you're all set!' screen with quick links to key features. the checklist should also gracefully collapse once done rather than just disappearing

started building the onboarding checklist component today. design question for the team — should the checklist items be hardcoded or configurable from the backend? @quinn_davis wants to A/B test different onboarding flows later, so data-driven seems like the way to go but it's more upfront work

definitely configurable — we're already discussing different onboarding paths for different user roles. the checklist items should come from the backend so we can experiment with the activation funnel without shipping new code each time
makes sense, will build the checklist as data-driven 👍

hey mike, grace walked me through the component structure yesterday which was super helpful. she mentioned I could pick up the empty states work for the onboarding redesign — is that a good first task? want to make sure I'm not biting off more than I can chew

empty states is a perfect first task! they're well-scoped and jack's wireframes are very detailed. start with the dashboard empty state — it's the simplest one. reference the existing EmptyState component in src/components/common/ and extend it with the new designs. let me know if you hit any blockers
awesome, thanks! I'll start with the dashboard empty state and work from jack's wireframes. will ping you if I get stuck

quick update — grace started on the onboarding checklist component, jordan is taking on empty states as a first task, and jack finalized the wireframes including the completion state. we're on track for the 2-week estimate. I'll have the onboarding spec fully documented by wednesday

great progress. make sure jordan has good support — this is their first real project contribution and I want the onboarding experience for our own new hires to be as good as the one we're building for users 😄

@jack_brown the onboarding checklist sidebar is taking shape — I have the expandable/collapsible behavior working with the progress bar. one thing I noticed in the wireframes: the checklist items don't have a 'skipped' state. should we add that? some users might want to skip optional steps

good catch — yes, let's add a skip option for non-critical checklist items. I'll update the wireframes with a 'skip for now' link and a way to revisit skipped items. the activation funnel should track skips separately from completions so we can see what people are avoiding
perfect, will add that to the component. thanks!

onboarding redesign spec is now live in the wiki. covers the full first-time experience: welcome screen wizard, onboarding checklist with configurable steps, product tour with anchored tooltips, and redesigned empty states. @sofia_martinez @priya_mehta please review when you get a chance — want to make sure the activation funnel instrumentation plan looks right

reviewed the onboarding spec — looks solid. one suggestion @quinn_davis: we should track time-to-complete for each wizard step, not just completion rates. if step 2 takes users 5 minutes but step 1 takes 30 seconds, that tells us where the friction is in the first-time experience

really good point — adding time-to-complete per step to the instrumentation plan. will also add a metric for tooltip engagement rate in the product tour so we can see which tooltips are actually being read vs dismissed

hey grace, I have the dashboard empty state mostly done following jack's wireframes. one thing I'm unsure about — should I use the existing Button component from the design system or is there a new variant for the onboarding CTAs? I saw something about primary-action buttons in the spec but couldn't find the component

use the existing Button with variant='primary' and size='lg' for the onboarding CTAs — that matches what jack has in the wireframes. the empty state illustrations are in the shared Figma assets, you can export them as SVGs. looking good so far! let me know when you have a PR up and I'll review
got it, thanks! will have the PR up by end of day

onboarding checklist component is done — configurable steps from the backend, progress tracking, skip functionality, collapsible sidebar. moving on to the welcome screen wizard next. @jordan_kim is also making good progress on empty states, should have a PR up today. @quinn_davis we're on schedule

amazing progress! the checklist was the foundation piece so having that done is huge. for the wizard, let's make sure each step has clear exit paths — we don't want users to feel trapped. they should be able to skip the wizard and come back to it from the checklist
will do. the wizard and checklist are connected — completing wizard steps automatically checks off the corresponding checklist items

reviewed @jordan_kim's empty state PR for the onboarding redesign — the dashboard one looks great, really close to the wireframes. left a few minor comments about spacing. for the projects page empty state, I think we need a slightly different illustration — the current one is too generic. I'll push an updated asset to Figma today
I'll pass that along to jordan — the spacing feedback is easy to fix. thanks for the quick review!

building the welcome screen wizard for the onboarding redesign — it's a multi-step form with animated transitions between steps. anyone know if we have a shared step-indicator component or should I build one from scratch? @eve_johnson @nina_scott wondering if this exists in the design system already

hey jack, I'm writing the product tour script — the sequence of tooltips that guide users through the interface after the wizard. can you review the copy and positioning? want to make sure the progressive disclosure feels natural and not overwhelming. doc is in the onboarding spec under 'product tour flow'

reviewed the product tour flow — the tooltip sequence makes sense. I'd suggest limiting it to 5 key tooltips max. user research consistently shows that tours longer than 5 steps have massive drop-off. we can cover the rest through contextual tooltips that appear on first interaction with each feature
agreed, trimming to 5 core tooltips. the contextual approach for everything else is much better for user activation anyway — learning in context rather than front-loading

just pushed the updated empty states PR with jack's feedback addressed — fixed the spacing and swapped in the new project page illustration. first PR at Bsus! 🎉 @grace_park it's ready for another look when you have time
congrats on the first PR jordan! 🎉 the empty states look really polished. this is going to make the first-time experience so much better for new users
nice work jordan! solid first contribution. the empty states are exactly what the onboarding flow needed — way better than the blank pages we had before

approved the empty states PR — looks great! merging now. next up if you're interested: the team page empty state still needs the same treatment, and then we could use help with the tooltip component for the product tour. let me know what sounds good
I'll grab the team page empty state since I have the pattern down now. the tooltip component sounds really interesting too — maybe I can shadow you on that one to learn?

welcome screen wizard is coming along — I have the 3-step flow working with transitions. the wizard integrates with the onboarding checklist so completing wizard steps checks off the corresponding items. working on the skip/dismiss flow next so users can bail out gracefully and pick up from the checklist later
sounds perfect. how's the tooltip system looking? that's the last major piece before we can do a full end-to-end test of the first-time experience

starting the tooltip overlay system tomorrow. jack's specs are detailed enough that it should go fast — anchored positioning with a backdrop dim, 5-step product tour, auto-advance on interaction. hoping to have it done by thursday

exec team loved the onboarding redesign plan. they want to see the user activation numbers two weeks after launch. @quinn_davis can we plan for a soft launch to 10% of new signups first so we can validate the funnel improvement before rolling out to everyone?

absolutely — staged rollout makes sense. I'll work with grace to put the whole new onboarding flow behind a feature flag. 10% → 50% → 100% based on activation funnel metrics. we'll need the analytics dashboard up before we start the rollout so we can monitor drop-off rates in real time

the product tour tooltip system for the onboarding redesign is working — anchored tooltips with backdrop dimming, keyboard navigation, 5-step guided flow. wrapping it behind a feature flag for staged rollout. @nina_scott I used the Tooltip primitive from the design system as a base and extended it — wanted to make sure that's cool and not going to conflict with anything in design-system-v2

we're getting close on the onboarding redesign — all major components are built. can you do a final design review of the whole flow? welcome screen → wizard → checklist → product tour → empty states. want to make sure the end-to-end first-time experience feels cohesive

on it — I'll do a thorough walkthrough today and flag anything that deviates from the wireframes. will also check that the progressive disclosure pacing feels right when you actually go through it as a new user. should have feedback by EOD

did the design review on the full onboarding flow — overall it's really close to the wireframes. a few tweaks: (1) the wizard step indicator needs more contrast, (2) the checklist completion animation is a bit fast — slow it down by ~200ms, (3) the product tour tooltip arrows are slightly off-center on smaller screens. nothing major, mostly polish
thanks for the thorough review! all easy fixes, will push them today. the responsive tooltip positioning was tricky — I'll add a viewport check to make sure arrows stay centered

onboarding redesign is feature complete! 🎉 new welcome screen wizard with progressive disclosure, configurable onboarding checklist, guided product tour with 5 anchored tooltips, redesigned empty states. rolling out to 10% of new signups next monday behind a feature flag. @sofia_martinez @priya_mehta we'll be watching the activation funnel drop-off metrics closely

incredible work by the whole onboarding redesign team — @quinn_davis @jack_brown @grace_park @jordan_kim. this is going to be a game changer for user activation. excited to see the funnel numbers once we go live

feature flag is set up and tested — new onboarding flow will activate for 10% of new signups starting monday. analytics events are instrumented at every step: wizard completion, checklist progress, tooltip engagement, empty state CTA clicks. we're ready

let's do this. really proud of what the team built — this is the most thoughtful first-time experience we've ever shipped. the user research, the progressive disclosure approach, the configurable checklist — it all came together beautifully. fingers crossed for the activation numbers 🤞
